chief_complaint: A 33-year-old female patient presented with severe iron deficiency anemia and significant abdominal pain.
history: The patient had prior negative gastroscopy and ileocolonoscopy findings. She underwent capsule endoscopy, which showed an ulcerated tumor-suspicious lesion in the jejunum. Biopsy samples obtained during jejunoscopy identified the lesion as a gastrointestinal stromal tumor.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided in the source.
labs: Iron deficiency anemia, fecal occult blood test positivity, capsule endoscopy showing an ulcerated tumor-suspicious lesion in the jejunum, biopsy samples confirming gastrointestinal stromal tumor.
